inputSchema.description: Empty params for tools that take no arguments.
inputSchema.title: EmptyParams

description: Attach to a notebook. Pass path (.ipynb) or notebook_id (UUID) — not both.

notebook_id.description:
UUID of a running notebook session from list_active_notebooks.
Either this OR path must be provided, not both.

path.description:
Canonical file path to open (e.g. "~/analysis.ipynb").
Either this OR notebook_id must be provided, not both.

name: connect_notebook

description: Create a notebook. Ephemeral by default; save_notebook(path) to persist.

environment_mode.description:
Environment source mode: "auto", "project", or "notebook".
Defaults to "auto".

ephemeral.description:
When true (default for MCP), notebook exists only in memory.
Use save_notebook(path=...) to persist to disk.

kernel.description: Alias for runtime (deprecated but supported for convenience).

package_manager.description:
Package manager for dependencies: "uv", "conda", or "pixi".
Defaults to the user's default_python_env setting.

name: create_notebook

description: Save notebook to disk. For notebooks created with create_notebook(), you must provide a path.

path.description:
Path to save the notebook to (e.g., "~/analysis.ipynb").
Required for ephemeral notebooks created with create_notebook().
Omit to save to the notebook's existing file path.

name: save_notebook

description: Open the notebook in the nteract app for the user. Headless: returns a structured no-display reason.

notebook_id.description: Notebook ID to show. Defaults to current session's notebook.

name: show_notebook

description: Release a notebook session's peer connection. Omit notebook_id to disconnect the active session.

notebook_id.description:
Notebook ID to disconnect and release. If omitted, disconnects the
active session. Pass a notebook_id to release a specific parked session
without switching away from the current one.

name: disconnect_notebook

description: Create a cell anchored by after_cell_id; omit after_cell_id to append.

after_cell_id.description: Insert after this cell. Omit to append at the end; pass null to insert at the beginning.

and_run.description:
Execute the cell immediately after creation. Response includes the
execution_id plus a compact output summary when the cell is code.

source.description: Cell source code or markdown content.

timeout_secs.description: Max seconds to wait for execution.

name: create_cell

description: Replace a cell's source or type, optionally executing it and returning execution_id/output summary.

and_run.description:
Execute the cell after changes (code cells only). Response includes
the execution_id plus a compact output summary.

cell_type.description: New cell type (None to leave unchanged).

source.description: New source code (None to leave unchanged).

timeout_secs.description: Max seconds to wait for execution.

name: set_cell

description: Move a cell anchored by after_cell_id; null/omitted after_cell_id means start.

after_cell_id.description: Move after this cell, or null for start.

name: move_cell

description: Execute a code cell and return execution_id, status, and compact output summary.

timeout_secs.description: Max seconds to wait; returns execution_id and partial results if exceeded.

name: execute_cell

description: Execute all code cells in order.

timeout_secs.description: Max seconds to wait for all cells to finish. Default: 300.

wait.description:
If true (default), wait for all cells to finish and return outputs.
If false, queue cells and return immediately.

name: run_all_cells

description: Get outputs and status (done/error/running/queued) for an execution_id.

execution_id.description:
The execution ID returned by `execute_cell`, `set_cell(and_run=true)`,
`create_cell(and_run=true)`, or `run_all_cells`.

full_output.description:
Return unabridged output text (like `get_cell(full_output=true)`).
Default: false (preview mode to protect context budget).

name: get_results

inputSchema.description: Empty params for tools that take no arguments.
inputSchema.title: EmptyParams

description: Restart the kernel, clearing all state.

inputSchema.description: Empty params for tools that take no arguments.
inputSchema.title: EmptyParams

name: restart_kernel

description: Review or update notebook dependencies. Returns current deps, fingerprint, and trust state.

add.description: Packages to add to the notebook's current dependency manager.

apply.description:
Action after dependency edits: "none" (default), "sync" (hot-install,
UV only), or "restart" (restart kernel with new deps).

dependency_fingerprint.description:
Optional fingerprint from a prior manage_dependencies or get_dependencies
response. With add/remove, this must match the pre-change dependency
fingerprint. Without add/remove, this must match the approved fingerprint.

remove.description: Packages to remove from the notebook's current dependency manager.

trust.description:
Approve and sign the resulting dependency metadata. With add/remove this
trusts the post-change dependency set. Without add/remove this approves
the current dependency set.

name: manage_dependencies

description: Replace literal text in a cell. Use context_before/context_after to disambiguate repeated matches.

and_run.description: Execute the cell immediately after edit.

context_after.description: Text that must appear after the match.

context_before.description: Text that must appear before the match.

match.description: Literal text to find (must match exactly once).

timeout_secs.description: Max seconds to wait for execution.

name: replace_match

description: Replace a regex match in a cell (fancy-regex). Fails if 0 or >1 matches. Replacement is literal text.

and_run.description: Execute the cell immediately after edit.

content.description: Literal replacement text — not interpreted as a regex or escape sequence. To insert a newline, use an actual newline character in the JSON string.

pattern.description: Regex pattern (must match exactly once). MULTILINE ((?m)) enabled by default — ^/$ match line boundaries, use \z for end-of-string. DOTALL is off — . does not match \n unless you add (?s).

timeout_secs.description: Max seconds to wait for execution.

name: replace_regex
